jokes.text: ♪ Cleanup, cleanup ♪ ♪ do your own housework, you little crackers ♪ - what? - My kindergarten teacher was a former black panther.
jokes.setup: Innocent children's cleanup song with unexpected racial slur
jokes.punchline: Revealed to be from a Black Panther kindergarten teacher
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of innocent children's song with dark political twist. Perfect economy and unexpected setup/payoff.

jokes.text: You look like you're in a gay porno.
jokes.setup: Criss looking sweaty with tool belt and 90s haircut
jokes.punchline: Specific visual comparison to gay porn aesthetic
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz observation with good visual specificity, though fairly predictable setup.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah? You're one to talk, buddy. I'm Brent. I'm the new guy on the crew. I'm Rod. I'm the boss. And what I say goes.
jokes.setup: Criss defending himself from gay porn comment
jokes.punchline: Immediately launches into actual gay porn dialogue
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that proves Liz's point by immediately embodying the gay porn trope. Meta and self-aware.

jokes.text: I've never been with a man before. Looks like I'm the boss now... Rod.
jokes.punchline: Full commitment to the bit with power dynamic reversal
jokes.explanation: Good follow-through on the established bit with role reversal twist.

jokes.text: Oh! What are we doing? - I don't know. Whatever! Let's wrestle! It doesn't matter.
jokes.setup: Gay porn roleplay getting out of hand
jokes.punchline: Desperate attempt to salvage the situation with generic wrestling
jokes.explanation: Natural conclusion to the escalating discomfort, though less clever than the setup.

jokes.text: You have a baby name book, and you circled Adolf?
jokes.setup: Finding adoption materials and baby name book
jokes.punchline: Discovery of Hitler's name circled as potential baby name
jokes.explanation: Shocking dark humor with perfect economy. The specificity of 'circled' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Actually, I borrowed that book from my mother, so be glad I'm not a boy.
jokes.setup: Criss horrified about Adolf being circled
jokes.punchline: Implies her mother would have named a boy Adolf
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that makes the original joke even darker. Brilliant economy and character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Murphy brown lied to us.
jokes.setup: Liz struggling with work-life balance and parenting
jokes.punchline: Blaming fictional TV character for unrealistic expectations
jokes.explanation: Great meta-commentary on unrealistic TV portrayals of working motherhood. The episode title reference.

jokes.text: She had Eldin.
jokes.punchline: Reference to Murphy Brown's live-in handyman/nanny
jokes.explanation: Good callback to specific Murphy Brown detail, though requires deeper show knowledge.

jokes.text: I keep getting my neighborallure magazine.
jokes.setup: Listing things she loves about her life
jokes.punchline: Fake magazine name combining 'neighbor' and 'allure'
jokes.explanation: Decent throwaway fake magazine name, typical 30 Rock background gag.

jokes.text: 'cause nunchaku-ing can wear a guy out
jokes.setup: Jack's patriotic speech about fighting back
jokes.punchline: Treating nunchucks as a verb with genuine physical concern
jokes.explanation: Absurd verbification of nunchucks with deadpan delivery. Very Jack Donaghy.

jokes.text: We have been creating and solving this country's problems for 200 years. Where's our history month?
jokes.punchline: Asking for history month when they already dominate all history
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment highlighting privilege blindness with great irony. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: which costs... I don't know, $90 a gallon?
jokes.setup: Jack talking about working families buying milk
jokes.punchline: Reveals complete disconnect from regular people's reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment showing his wealth-induced disconnection from reality.

jokes.text: Raymour and his conjoined twin, Flanigan.
jokes.punchline: Treating furniture store name as literal conjoined twins
jokes.explanation: Brilliant literalization of a furniture store name. Absurd but delivered matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: Ashley of Ashley furniture will be there, and he is a genitally androgynous pinhead.
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and inappropriate personal details
jokes.explanation: Jack's inappropriate oversharing about business contacts. Character-specific inappropriateness.

jokes.text: I hope success isn't far, far away.
jokes.punchline: Setting up Star Wars reference
jokes.explanation: Weak setup for Star Wars joke. Forced and predictable pun attempt.

jokes.text: What aboutstar wars do you want to say, lemon?
jokes.punchline: Jack immediately calling out the forced reference
jokes.explanation: Meta-comedy calling out the previous weak joke attempt. Self-aware and clever.

jokes.text: School, church, alone on the playground, a child therapist's office.
jokes.setup: Listing places a girl can wear Princess Leia costume
jokes.punchline: Escalating to increasingly inappropriate/sad venues
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list that gets darker with each item. Perfect structure.

jokes.text: Nixon's been dead almost 20 years, not in cryogenic hibernation, resting up for 2016.
jokes.setup: Explaining her dad signed Nixon biography as joke
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific denial of Nixon conspiracy theory
jokes.explanation: Absurd over-explanation that creates its own conspiracy theory. Good specificity.

jokes.text: Has anyone ever known a good person named Kevin?
jokes.setup: Jack trying to set her up with someone named Kevin
jokes.punchline: Universal distrust of people named Kevin
jokes.explanation: Relatable observation with good economy. Sets up Jack's counter-examples.

jokes.text: I'm thinking Sonny and... Cher.
jokes.punchline: Jack immediately knows where she's going and completes thought
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing their relationship dynamic.

jokes.text: Kevin garnett helped me move once. Kevin costner cooked me dinner after a bad breakup. Kevin Sorbo intRoduced me to his podiatrist.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and absurd celebrity Kevin interactions
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation ending with Kevin Sorbo podiatrist introduction. Very Jack Donaghy name-dropping.

jokes.text: I was busy burning down the panda express that I had fallen in love with.
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions arson over emotional attachment to chain restaurant
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Jenna moment. The casual delivery of arson makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Something I wish I'd known before I released my sex tape with the six flags guy.
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions sex tape with amusement park mascot
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna inappropriate overshare with specific absurd detail.

jokes.text: "Da-mi", "de-mi", Heather, Lindsay, Britney, Muammar... so sad. A life cut short.
jokes.punchline: Including Gaddafi in celebrity breakdown list
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation placing dictator among pop stars. Dark humor with perfect timing.

jokes.text: So sad. A life cut short. He gave the best diamonds.
jokes.punchline: Mourning him for his diamond gifts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment - completely missing moral implications, focused on personal benefits.

jokes.text: Justin and K-fed, Bruce and Ashton, Samantha Ronson and Herbie the Lovebug.
jokes.punchline: Including fictional Volkswagen Beetle as romantic partner
jokes.explanation: Great escalation ending with absurd car romance. Perfect Jenna obliviousness.

jokes.text: They turned it into a training facility for single mothers to teach illegal immigrants how to fill out unemployment forms.
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific liberal government program
jokes.explanation: Good Jack conservative paranoia humor with specific absurd details.

jokes.text: We bought it at west elm.
jokes.setup: Jack asking how prototype couch was different
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals they just bought a store couch
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan reveal that undermines the entire project. Great economy.

jokes.text: All they teach us now is how to build rollercoasters and survivor challenges.
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific entertainment-focused curriculum
jokes.explanation: Good observation about American priorities with specific absurd examples.

jokes.text: He baked those rocks and sold them to children. As gum.
jokes.setup: Jack telling inspirational Bazooka Joe story
jokes.punchline: Casually describes selling rocks as chewing gum
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd origin story delivered with complete sincerity. Perfect economy with the pause.

jokes.text: I cannot give it to you. I'm a married man. But more importantly, I find you very unattractive.
jokes.punchline: Tracy mishears as sexual proposition and adds insult
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy misdirection with characteristic blunt insult. Classic character moment.

jokes.text: Partying too hard with Christian slater? Furious about Nafta? Y2k panic? My heyday was the '90s.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly dated references ending with self-aware admission
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list of 90s references with self-aware punchline. Very Tracy.

jokes.text: He started by being boring and ruining my anniversary, 'cause Angie wanted to take advantage of "all the great theatre in New York".
jokes.punchline: Personal grievance about Shakespeare ruining date night
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy perspective on high culture through personal grievance lens.

jokes.text: 'Cause those guys always put fuel in my tank.
jokes.setup: Wanting to watch Mythbusters before sex
jokes.punchline: Mechanical metaphor for sexual arousal
jokes.explanation: Decent Liz sexual humor with mechanical metaphor fitting the show.

jokes.text: "Sent from one of my four iPads"?
jokes.punchline: Absurd wealth display through multiple tablets
jokes.explanation: Decent observation about obnoxious wealth signaling through technology.

jokes.text: "Dudely, let's make coffee our bitch."
jokes.setup: Liz writing back to Kevin in his language
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt at bro-speak
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor showing Liz's awkward attempt at modern slang.

jokes.text: You know what this country used to sit on? Logs. Girders. Poles.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly uncomfortable seating options as American ideal
jokes.explanation: Great Jack American nostalgia parody with good escalation.

jokes.text: Kouchtown. Sit down or get out of the way.
jokes.punchline: Aggressive ultimatum as furniture marketing
jokes.explanation: Decent furniture slogan parody with appropriate aggression for Jack's brand.

jokes.text: I can see the veins behind my eyes. Oh! I can see pain!
jokes.punchline: Escalating descriptions of discomfort to philosophical
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy escalation from physical to abstract pain.

jokes.text: The design forces the sitter into what Israeli military interrogators call a stress position.
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to torture techniques
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with Jack's casual knowledge of interrogation techniques.

jokes.text: It comes in espresso, dandelion, putty, and, as you see here, lagoon.
jokes.punchline: Normal furniture color options for torture device
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan delivery treating torture device as normal product.

jokes.text: You look like a substitute teacher who just got her one-year a.A. Chip.
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific insult combining education and addiction recovery
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific Jack insult combining multiple forms of modest achievement.

jokes.text: Dhanalakshmi, I'm happy for you, and I'm gonna let you finish, but I just gotta say, Gurubarath Kurrupuswami spelled one of the toughest words of all time!
jokes.punchline: Kanye West interruption meme applied to spelling bee
jokes.explanation: Decent meme application but relies on existing format rather than original humor.

jokes.text: Then a kleptomania relapse ending in orgasm and arrest.
jokes.punchline: Combining shoplifting, sexual climax, and legal consequences
jokes.explanation: Efficient escalation combining multiple absurd elements. Good economy.

jokes.text: Me running through that window.
jokes.punchline: Immediate destruction as her next career move
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of breakdown performance with immediate payoff.

jokes.text: Where I'm from, Uncle Sam's mouth is sewn up, and then he's set on fire, so I don't know how he talks.
jokes.punchline: Casual description of Uncle Sam torture/execution ritual
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth moment revealing disturbing Southern traditions with innocent delivery.

jokes.text: This couch... Is a failure. I hate golf. One time in college, I smoked a clove cigarette. I keep buying candles as gifts and keeping them for myself. My natural hair color is bright red.
jokes.setup: Jack tortured by couch into confessing
jokes.punchline: Escalating from business failure to increasingly personal secrets
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation revealing Jack's hidden vulnerabilities. Perfect character work.

jokes.text: And will you accept dancing as money?
jokes.punchline: Proposing interpretive dance as currency
jokes.explanation: Classic Kenneth otherworldly economic understanding with perfect delivery.

jokes.text: A media-savvy crackhead, I know.
jokes.punchline: Self-aware description of his social media skills
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy self-awareness about his media savvy and drug use.

jokes.text: I can see that you're on a skateboard, David. This is why we broke up, Jenna.
jokes.setup: David Blaine visiting during Jenna's breakdown
jokes.punchline: Magic trick ruined by visible skateboard, relationship commentary
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag deflating magic trick pretension with relationship dynamics.

jokes.text: At one point, your veneers fell off, and you had these little baby fangs.
jokes.punchline: Disturbing intimate detail about dental work failure
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor with specific grotesque detail. Character-appropriate overshare.

jokes.text: you were the only one who shot my wife.
jokes.setup: Frank talking about students he dated
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions Jenna shooting his spouse
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark escalation delivered with complete casualness. Shocking but earned.

jokes.text: It's patronizing. Like girls don't know what jobs are.
jokes.setup: Objecting to take your daughter to work day
jokes.punchline: Sharp feminist critique from 11-year-old
jokes.explanation: Great smart kid humor with legitimate social critique.

jokes.text: I had my first crush on a shop teacher who looked just like Boo Radley.
jokes.punchline: Romantic attraction to reclusive possibly dangerous character
jokes.explanation: Good Liz romantic dysfunction with literary reference.

jokes.text: but they wanted to call me shorts accident.
jokes.punchline: Team preferred reference to embarrassing pants incident
jokes.explanation: Good Liz backstory revealing another embarrassing high school moment.

jokes.text: So we settled on Supervirgin.
jokes.punchline: Landing on even worse nickname than original attempt
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz high school awkwardness with escalating bad nicknames.

jokes.text: So we settled on supervirgin.
jokes.punchline: Compromise nickname that's even worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation where the compromise is actually worse. Great Liz character work.

jokes.text: but I can negotiate up from puked in thermos.
jokes.setup: Cat discussing her current nickname situation
jokes.punchline: Reveals even worse current nickname involving vomit
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Cat's pragmatic approach to terrible nicknames.

jokes.text: That people who talk the most in meetings often know the least. She sat in two meetings with me. I crushed it.
jokes.punchline: Kevin immediately exemplifies what she just criticized
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic structure with Kevin proving his daughter's point immediately.

jokes.text: Either the wicked queen inDisney on ice or the third woman president.
jokes.punchline: Combining performance villain with historical achievement
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of silly and serious aspirations. Age-appropriate humor.

jokes.text: And he's in trouble, too, 'cause I watched some boxercise videos on demand before coming down here.
jokes.punchline: His fighting preparation is exercise videos
jokes.explanation: Good Criss character moment showing his non-threatening nature.

jokes.text: game go is not a phrase.
jokes.punchline: Liz immediately calling out the nonsensical phrase
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment calling out nonsense dialogue. Self-aware writing.

jokes.text: You tried to dine at Balthazar without a reservation!
jokes.punchline: Restaurant reservation as peak insanity
jokes.explanation: Perfect celebrity perspective where restaurant etiquette is serious transgression.

jokes.text: You mean our grave. Where we'll lie on top of each other in one coffin, pelvis to face, for eternity.
jokes.setup: Jenna talking about taking regret to her grave
jokes.punchline: Tracy's disturbing vision of their shared eternal resting position
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly disturbing escalation with specific grotesque imagery. Perfectly Tracy.

jokes.text: Oh, you are being so trans-vaginal right now.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining women can choose to be childless
jokes.punchline: Jack using medical term as general insult
jokes.explanation: Good Jack moment using inappropriate medical terminology as insult. Character-specific.

jokes.text: Sink them and make a reef to protect gay turtles?
jokes.setup: Wondering what government will do with couches
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific liberal environmental program
jokes.explanation: Good Jack paranoia about liberal government waste with absurd specificity.

jokes.text: Sink them and make a reef to protect gay turtles?
jokes.setup: Wondering what government will do with couches
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific environmental program for gay turtles
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist Jack paranoia about liberal government programs.

jokes.text: Tree bark. Glass. Shotgun shells. The broken swords of our vanquished enemies.
jokes.setup: Jack lamenting soft gum in commercial
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of increasingly violent/medieval chewing materials
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation ending with medieval conquest reference. Classic Jack tough-guy fantasy.

jokes.text: It's like chewing a Mountain that someone shot a freeze ray into.
jokes.punchline: Absurd sci-fi comparison for gum hardness
jokes.explanation: Great absurd comparison with specific sci-fi imagery. Good callback to the fake gum origin story.
